workflow.purpose: Interactive state-checker, settings configurator, and guided router for project initialization. Detects which ACE documents exist, ensures settings are configured (including GitHub Project integration), displays a status dashboard, and offers to run missing setup commands — following the GSD new-project interactive pattern.
workflow.mandatory-context: Read all files referenced by the invoking prompt's execution-context before starting.

process.step:
**MANDATORY FIRST STEP — Execute environment detection before any user interaction.**
```bash INIT=$(node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js init new-project) ```
Whether .docs/product/product-vision.md exists Whether .docs/wiki/system-wide/system-architecture.md exists Whether .docs/wiki/system-wide/system-structure.md exists Whether .docs/wiki/system-wide/coding-standards.md exists Whether .docs/wiki/system-wide/testing-framework.md exists Existing code or package files detected No existing code detected Whether .git directory exists Whether GitHub CLI (gh) is installed
```bash git init ```
No action needed.

**Create `.ace/settings.json` if it does not exist.**
```bash SETTINGS=$(node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js ensure-settings) ```
Whether settings.json was just created (true) or already existed (false) The current settings object
Display: ``` ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ACE > Settings │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
✓ Created .ace/settings.json with defaults. ```
No display needed — settings already exist.

**Detect GitHub environment and ask the user about project board integration.**
```bash GH_STATUS=$(node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js init setup-github) ```
Whether GitHub CLI (gh) is available Detected repository (e.g., owner/repo) or empty string Repository owner or empty string Array of {number, title} objects Current github_project settings from settings.json
Already configured. Display: ``` ✓ GitHub Project: #{current_settings.project_number} Owner: {current_settings.owner} Repo: {current_settings.repo} ``` Skip to step 4.
Display: ``` i GitHub CLI (gh) not found — GitHub Project integration unavailable. Install from: https://cli.github.com/ ``` Skip to step 4.
Continue to substep 3.3.
Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "GitHub" - question: "GitHub CLI detected. Do you want to connect a GitHub Project board for sprint tracking?" - options: - "Yes, set it up (Recommended)" — Connect a GitHub Project now - "Skip for now" — Continue without GitHub Project integration
Skip to step 4.
Continue to substep 3.4.
Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Repository" - question: "Detected repository: `{repo}`. Is this correct?" - options: - "Yes, use {repo}" — Confirm this repository - "No, different repo" — I'll provide the correct repository
If "No, different repo": ask user to provide the correct `owner/repo` string. Parse `owner` from the confirmed/provided repo string (everything before `/`).
Ask the user to provide their `owner/repo` string. Parse `owner` from the provided string (everything before `/`).
The `owner` is the GitHub user or organization that owns the project board. By default it matches the repo owner, but it can differ (e.g., org project boards used across multiple repos).
Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Owner" - question: "Which GitHub user or organization owns the project board? Detected: `{owner}`" - options: - "Use {owner}" — The repo owner owns the project board - "Different owner" — I'll provide the correct owner
Keep `owner` as-is.
Ask user to provide the correct owner (GitHub username or org name). Update `owner` with the provided value.
Re-fetch projects for the confirmed owner (in case owner changed in 3.5): ```bash gh project list --owner {owner} --limit 10 --format json ```
Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Project" - question: "Which GitHub Project should ACE use?" - options: (build from projects array, max 4) - "{title} (#{number})" — for each project
Display: ``` i No GitHub Projects found for {owner}. Create one at https://github.com/orgs/{owner}/projects or https://github.com/users/{owner}/projects ``` Skip to step 4.
```bash node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js write-github-settings enabled=true gh_installed=true repo={repo} project_number={number} owner={owner} ```
Display: ``` ✓ GitHub Project configured. Owner: {owner} Repo: {repo} Project: {title} (#{number}) ```

**MANDATORY — MUST execute this step. DO NOT skip. Always ask the user about Agent Teams.**
This step syncs and configures the Claude Code Agent Teams experimental feature. You MUST run the sync command below and MUST use AskUserQuestion to interact with the user.
**MANDATORY — Run this command before evaluating state:**
```bash AGENT_TEAMS=$(node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js sync-agent-teams --raw) ```
Parse the JSON output. The `agent_teams` field is the synced boolean state.
**MANDATORY — You MUST use AskUserQuestion here. Do NOT skip this interaction.** **IMPORTANT: When writing settings, you MUST use the Bash commands below. NEVER directly edit .ace/settings.json or .claude/settings.json — the write-agent-teams command updates BOTH files atomically.**
Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Agent Teams" - question: "Agent Teams (experimental) is currently ENABLED. This orchestrates multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel but significantly increases token usage. Would you like to keep it enabled?" - options: - "Keep enabled" — Continue with Agent Teams enabled - "Disable" — Turn off Agent Teams to reduce token usage
```bash node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js write-agent-teams false ``` Display: ``` ✓ Agent Teams disabled. ```
Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Agent Teams" - question: "Would you like to enable Claude Code Agent Teams? This is an experimental feature that orchestrates multiple Claude Code sessions working in parallel. WARNING: This significantly increases token usage." - options: - "Skip for now (Recommended)" — Keep Agent Teams disabled - "Enable Agent Teams" — Enable experimental multi-session orchestration (higher token cost)
```bash node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js write-agent-teams true ``` Display: ``` ✓ Agent Teams enabled. - Set agent_teams: true in .ace/settings.json - Set CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS in .claude/settings.json ⚠ This feature is experimental and increases token usage significantly. ```

step.name: offer-map-system
step.condition: is_brownfield is true AND has_system_architecture is false
step.substep: Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Codebase" - question: "I detected existing code but no wiki documentation. Would you like to map your codebase first?" - options: - "Map codebase (Recommended)" — Run /ace:map-system to analyze architecture, structure, and testing framework - "Skip for now" — Continue without mapping

substep.name: route-map-decision
substep.variant: Display: ``` Next > Run /ace:map-system to map your codebase. This will generate system-structure, system-architecture, and testing-framework documents. ``` **Exit workflow.**

step.name: offer-product-vision
step.substep: Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Vision" - question: "No product vision found. Would you like to define one now?" - options: - "Create product vision (Recommended)" — Run /ace:plan-product-vision to define what you're building and why - "Skip for now" — Continue without a product vision

substep.name: route-vision-decision
substep.variant: Display: ``` Next > Run /ace:plan-product-vision to define your product vision. This drives all downstream planning. ``` **Exit workflow.**

step.name: offer-coding-standards
step.substep: Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Standards" - question: "No coding standards found. Would you like to generate them?" - options: - "Generate coding standards (Recommended)" — Run /ace:init-coding-standards to create tailored standards - "Skip for now" — Continue without coding standards

substep.name: route-standards-decision
substep.variant: Display: ``` Next > Run /ace:init-coding-standards to generate tailored coding standards. ``` **Exit workflow.**

step.name: offer-product-backlog
step.substep:
```bash HAS_BACKLOG=$(node ~/.claude/agile-context-engineering/src/ace-tools.js verify-path-exists .ace/artifacts/product/product-backlog.md --raw) ```
Backlog already exists. Skip to step 10.
Continue to substep 8.2.

Use `AskUserQuestion`: - header: "Backlog" - question: "You have a product vision but no backlog. Would you like to plan your product backlog?" - options: - "Plan backlog (Recommended)" — Run /ace:plan-backlog to break your vision into epics and features - "Skip for now" — Continue without a backlog

substep.name: route-backlog-decision
substep.variant: Display: ``` Next > Run /ace:plan-backlog to break your vision into epics and features. ``` **Exit workflow.**

step.name: completion

substep.name: greenfield-note
substep.condition: is_greenfield is true AND has_system_architecture is false

step.substep: Display: ``` i System architecture, structure, and testing framework docs are created by /ace:map-system after you have code to analyze. ```

substep.name: fully-initialized

variant.condition: all documents exist (vision + architecture + structure + coding standards + testing framework)

substep.variant:
Display: ``` ✓ Project is fully initialized. All core documents are in place.
Next > /ace:plan-project Start planning your product backlog. ```

variant.condition: some documents are missing (user skipped offers)

substep.variant: Display: ``` i Run /ace:help anytime to check status and pick up where you left off. ```

success_criteria.check: GitHub Project integration offered to the user (not silently skipped)
Agent Teams: sync-agent-teams command executed AND AskUserQuestion presented (NEVER skip this)
Status dashboard displayed showing all document states, settings, and GitHub status
Missing setup commands offered interactively in priority order
No document creation performed (delegated to specialized commands)
